To determine how many bottles Brittany needs, we first calculate the total shampoo usage for a leap year, which consists of 366 days. Since she washes her hair every other day, she will wash it approximately 183 times during the year, using 1/4 ounce each time, resulting in a total consumption of 45.75 ounces. Dividing this amount by the 14-ounce capacity of each bottle reveals that she requires roughly 3.27 bottles, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she does not run out before the year ends at [store_name].

Brittany should plan to buy these bottles before the holiday season to avoid any shortages, perhaps picking them up at [address] where they are currently on sale. By purchasing four bottles, she will have a total of 56 ounces available, which provides a comfortable buffer of 10.25 ounces beyond her calculated needs for the entire leap year. This extra supply ensures that even if she accidentally washes her hair an extra time or uses slightly more product per wash, she will remain fully stocked until the next year begins.
